items.title: Large Reasoning Models and Agentic Reasoning
items.description: LLMs, Large Reasoning Models, Agentic Reasoning.

items.title: From Numbers to Meaning: Machine Learning, Deep Neural Networks, Large Language Models, and Multimodal Generative AI
items.description: Introductory course for doctora students to provide a base understanding of concepts like Machine Learning, Deep Neural Networks, Large Language Models, and Multimodal Generative AI.

items.title: Explainable AI and Machine Learning: Ethical and Unbiased Decision-Making
items.description: XAI methods (post-hoc, ante-hoc, global vs. local), SHAP, LIME, PDPs, and ethical aspects of explainable AI.

items.title: AI, Reasoning, and Decisions
items.description: Case studies and real-world AI applications, including guest lecture by Prof. Dov Gabbay on symbolic AI and reasoning.

items.title: Argumentation and Case-Based Reasoning for Cognitive Behavioural Therapy with Generative AI
items.description: Using LLMs to build logically coherent chatbots with cognitive-behavioural therapy skills.

items.title: Generative AI, Multimodal Foundation Models, and Logically Consistent Artificial Agents
items.description: Generative AI, multimodality in large models, and symbolic (knowledge-driven) constraints for artificial agents.

items.title: Lecture on Large Language Models
items.description: Technical foundations of LLMs: training, fine-tuning, alignment, and real-world applications — invited at a Master's course.

items.title: Input/Output Logic
items.description: Class on deontic logic and input/output logic, part of a regular Master's degree course.

items.title: Elements of AI — Neural Networks Module
items.description: Perceptron, backpropagation, non-linear functions, biases, and weights.

items.title: Tree Kernels Methodologies
items.description: ML and NLP classification methodologies with a focus on Tree Kernels and statistical methods — invited lecture as part of the curricular activity.

items.title: Data Mining using Python
items.description: Data mining techniques using NumPy, pandas, and scikit-learn, for Master's students in Digital Humanities.

items.thesis: A Knowledge-Driven Framework for Multilingual Legal Harmonization Using Large Language Models
items.programme: PhD in Informatique · University of Luxembourg (cotutelle with University of Bologna)
items.supervisors: Luigi Di Caro (University of Turin), Réka Markovich (University of Luxembourg)

items.programme: PhD in Informatique · University of Luxembourg (cotutelle with University of Bologna)
items.supervisors: Monica Palmirani (University of Bologna), Leon van der Torre (University of Luxembourg)

items.thesis: An Empirical Study on Aligning Retrieval-Augmented Language Models as AI Health Coach (A Comparative Analysis of SFT and Base Model Selection)
items.programme: Master in Information and Computer Science (MICS) · University of Luxembourg

items.name: Guillaume B***
items.thesis: How LLMs Can Be Exploited and a Potential Framework SENATLLM to Undermine the Attacks
items.programme: Master in Information and Computer Science (MICS) · University of Luxembourg

items.name: Gabriel S***
items.thesis: Testing large models' strategic reasoning using multi-agent systems
items.programme: Master in Information and Computer Science (MICS) · University of Luxembourg
